channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Influx of graffiti along Stockwell main road SW9, 19th April
item.description:
I like to report an influx of graffiti along Stockwell main road SW9. The graffiti is very visible its on the walls to building entrances. It's also on the street boxes along the whole stretch. There is lastly graffiti directly outside Stockwell underground station sprayed on the right hand side of the wall. Please can the graffiti be removed The graffiti has recently been put there approximately over a week.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Stockwell Road, Stockwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping, 19th April
item.description:
Chairs have been left on the pavement for at least 4 days outside 91, Old Woolwich Rd

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Old Woolwich Road, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Base of Tree Blocks Footpath, 19th April
item.description:
The base of this tree is always sprouting new shoots which block the footpath and I suspect the base of the tree itself has now encroached into the footpath.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Haldane Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Illuminated sign - rusty, 19th April
item.description:
Sign extension arm and lantern arm are all rusty
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 24, Lynton Avenue, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overfilled rubbish bins. Food in bags dragged out by animals., 19th April
item.description:
Wheelie bins outside 138 Westcombe Hill are over-filled so that lids do not close. Often bags containing food stick out and at night foxes pull them out. This morning when I went out to take my bins up the steps for the Monday collection, I saw that a bag I'd seen sticking out of a bin had been dragged down my steps by foxes, pulled apart, food remains spread about and the foxes had fouled on top of it. I had to clean up this mess. Please could you provide larger or extra bins to the three tenants of 138 and write to them pointing out the problem overfilled bins causes, and asking them to ensure bins are not overfilled so that lids cannot close, to prevent animals doing this. Thanking you.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Westcombe Hill, Blackheath
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Railings loose and dangerous, 19th April
item.description:
The railings are loose and not tied or connected to anything. The fell over earlier and are very heavy. They need to be secured again. They are dangerous at present.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Pownall Road, Hackney, London

item.title: Confusing Sign, 19th April
item.description:
Sign that indicated the LTN status as inactive is half fallen down. This is very confusing for motorists and may result in inaccurate penalties.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Palace Road, Tulse Hill, Greater London

item.title: Twisted no entry sign at the entrance to a cycle lane safety hazard, 19th April
item.description:
The ‘No Entry Except Cycles’ sign at the entry to this one-way street has been twisted out of alignment — the angle and position suggest deliberate interference rather than accidental impact. As a result, the sign is no longer clearly visible to approaching drivers. I have noticed that 2 weeks ago and below is the summary of the observed situation> The consequences are already visible on the ground: cars are regularly entering the street against the permitted direction of traffic. The street is narrow, carries a two-way contraflow cycle lane, and is used by pedestrians crossing on foot. Wrong-way drivers pose a direct collision risk to cyclists travelling legally in the contraflow lane, and a risk to pedestrians who reasonably do not expect vehicles coming from that direction. I would ask the council to: 1. Restore and secure the ‘No Entry Except Cycles’ sign urgently, and inspect for signs of tampering. 2. Add a painted ‘No Entry’ roundel on the carriageway at the entry point — a standard, low-cost reinforcement that remains visible even if signage is interfered with again. 3. Assess this location for ANPR or moving traffic camera enforcement under the powers granted to London boroughs by Part 6 of the Traffic Management Act 2004. Observable contravention rates here are high enough that enforcement would be self-funding while materially reducing risk to cyclists and pedestrians. This is not a cosmetic issue — a serious collision at this location is foreseeable. I would appreciate a reference number and confirmation of when the signage will be restored. Kind regards, Michal Radzio

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Pilgrimage Street, The Borough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Glengall road, 19th April

item.title: Flytipping, 19th April
